docs.title: John's 2026 ROOK MK2
docs.text:
Welcome...

These are my modificaitions of Rolohauns Rook MK2 3D printer!. If you want the official version visit that link.

docs.title: Specifications
docs.text:
• 120 x120mm Build Area
  • 4:1 Gear Reduction Z
  • Cartesian Motion for simplicity
  • Fully 3D printed Frame
  • Dragonburner Toolhead with wide extruder and hotend support
  • Sensorless and Endstop homing support

This is my documentation. There are many like it, but this one is mine.

My documentation is my best friend. It is my life. I must master it as I must master my printer.

Without my printer, my documentation is useless. Without my documentation, my printer is useless. I must explain every part clearly. I must illustrate better than the confusion that threatens to jam my extruder. I must guide the build before frustration sets in.

I will show every step. I will label every screw, rod, and pulley. I will document every wire, sensor, and motor. I will include images, notes, and tips that prevent mistakes.

I am the master of my documentation. I am the guardian of clarity. I am the protector of the build.

My documentation, with me, is powerful. It will instruct. It will prevent errors. It will save hours of trial and frustration. I will keep it up to date. I will keep it precise. I will keep it accessible.

I will never leave a step ambiguous. I will never skip a warning. I will always maintain it.

This is my documentation. There are many like it, but this one is mine.

docs.title: Warning
docs.text:
Early Release

This is Version 0 of this manual. The printer is not finished and neither is this.

docs.title: 2. Attach rail and sides to gantry
Add pin pulley and motors to gantry
Add the rest of the pins and pulleys
Add the top of the frame
mount bed holder to rails (also attach endstop bar)
insert springs and mount bed to holder
Belts
docs.text:
The Rook MK2 uses Cartesian motion with belts and apex clips to move the print head and build platform. Proper belt installation is essential for accurate and smooth motion. All belts attach using apex clips, which lock the belt in place without requiring screws.

Note

This guide applies to all axes (Both X, Y, and Z). The belt installation method is the same for each.

  1. Route the belt around the motor pulley, idler, and guide as shown in the frame diagram.
  2. Attach the belt using apex clip to one side:
    • Insert the belt end fully into the provide hole, making sure the teeth align properly to the hole.
    • Insert the apex clip on the oposite side.
    • Press firmly until the clip clicks into place.
  3. Route the belt through the pulley tensioner and to the other side:
    • Make sure tensioner pulley if fully extended so you can later pull the tension tight.
    • Insert belt fully into the opposite hole making sure the teeth are oriented correctly.
    • Finally insert the apex clip securing the belt.
    • This allows you to adjust the belt tension later after fully assembled.
  4. Check basic movement:
    • Move the axis manually to ensure the belt runs smoothly and does not slip.
  5. Adjust tension once assembly is complete:
    • Use the screw to adjust the slide tensioner and fine-tune the belt tension until there is no slack but the motion remains smooth.

Tip

  • Align belt teeth carefully with pulleys to prevent skipping.
  • Avoid over-tightening; excessive tension can damage motors or pulleys.
  • Double-check all axes for smooth, free movement before final calibration.

docs.title: BOM
docs.text:
Go through this list and cross off anything you have. Then add up what is left is what your printer will cost.

Things that are not listed here are 3 partial spools of filament, your time, patience and last remaining hairs.

The purchasing suggestions here will have leftover parts to start your next project.

You may find some parts cheaper on Aliexpress if you want to wait a month.

docs.title: Shopping list
docs.text: Qty Hardware 88 Heatsets M3x5x4 $10 1 1600pcs M3 Screw Kit $18 1 443pcs M3 Long Screw Kit $10 3 MGN9C 200mm for Y and X $30 2 MGN9C 150mm for Z $20 4 F695 Bearing Pack of 10 $7 3 Nema17 42mm Any you prefer for Y and Z $11 1 Nema17 Pancake Stepper 23mm MAX Body Length for X $10 GT2 6mm wide Belt 6 meters $9 5 20 Tooth 5mm Bore Pulley $9 4 20 Tooth 5mm Bore Idler $10 1 80 Tooth Pulley for Z $10 1 188mm GT2 Belt Loop for Z (7mm tall with t) $15 2 5mm to 5mm bore coupler (Inner Diameter: 5mm-5mm(D14xL25)) $7 3 400mm CF or Steel Rod 5mm Diameter $10 4 Rubber Feet or print TPU SUB TOTAL ~$148 v0 BED Spring Steel & Magnet 120x120mm $15 Aluminum v0 Bed $20 v0 Heater $15 Bed Springs and M3 Bolts and M3 Locknuts or heatset $5 SUB TOTAL ~$50 Extruder + Hotend (Dragonburner) Bambu X1 Clone hotend $13 Sherpa Extruder $30 PC4-M10 Pneumatic Fittings $5 PTFE Tube $6 SUB TOTAL ~$50 Qty Electronics 1 24 Volt 6 Amp Power Supply AC Adapter $24 1 DC Power Jack Socket, 5.5mm x 2.5mm Female Barrel Panel Mount $7 2 or 3 Endstop Omron ( If not using sensorless homeing ) $6 optional BIQU Microprobe $26 urchoice BIGTREETECH DZ-01 MCU $85 1 Spool of wiring $18 1 630PCS JST PH Kit PH2.0mm Connectors Set $10 1 3010 24v ball bearing fan for hotend(included on hotend) $11 2 4010 24vball bearing fan for layer cooling $11 SUB TOTAL ~$161

docs.title: Part Prep
docs.text:
Cut the 5mm rod into these lengths:

  • Rod 1: 220mm + 60mm + 3x 40mm
  • Rod 2: 220mm + 3x 40mm + 2x 30mm
  • Rod 3: 5x40mm + 2x15mm

This will give you the following:

qty Rod Cut List purpose 2 220mm X Y motors 1 60mm Pin for Z 2 15mm Pin for Y Idlers 1 30mm Pin for X Idler 11 40mm Pin for frame

Cut the belt into these lengths:

Qty Belt Cut List purpose 1  mm Z 2 mm X 1  mm Y

These are the screws that you will need:

Qty Screw purpose 8 M3 x 6mm 29 M3 x 8mm 42 M3 x 10mm 9 M3 x 12mm 11 M3 x 16mm 4 M3 x 20mm 4 M3 x 25mm 13 M3 x 35mm 4 M5 x 30mm Qty JST purpose 4 6 pin female 4 wire to stepper motor  4 4 pin female 4 wire to mcu for stepper 3 2 pin female 2 wire to mcu for endstop

docs.title: Introduction
docs.text:
Warning

Assembling a 3D printer is a time consuming and dangerous process.

If you have not built anything before this project can kill you and may seriously hurt you.

This machine can maim, burn and electrocute you if you are not careful.

We take ZERO responsibility for your ability or education, this information is for educational purposes only.

Please, read and reread the entire manual fully and carefully before you start assembly.

NOTE: This is an unfinished beta machine so it may not even work as described.

docs.title: Parts
docs.text:
Info

This printer was designed so it could be printed with PLA and no heated bed, although I have not tested this. YMMV

I have printed my Rooks with various parts in various filaments. For example: PLA for skirts and trim, ASA for Bed mount and rails, and PETG for enclosure.

For best results print this printer using a Material type of ABS, ASA.

The following table is to help guide your decision making filament type choice.

Type Scale Factor Shrinkage Strength Impact Resistance Hardness / Rigidity Heat Resistance Aging Resistance Printability Cost ASA 100.50% 0.4% – 0.7% ~50–55 MPa High High (Shore D ~70) ~90–100c High Moderate (enclosure) Medium-High ABS 100.81% 0.7% – 1.6% ~40–50 Medium-High Medium-High ~85–100c Low Moderate (enclosure) Low-Medium PETG 100.40% 0.2% – 1% ~50–60 High Medium ~75–85c Medium Easy Medium PLA 100.30% 0.2% – 0.5% ~45–60 Low-Medium High, but brittle ~60c Low Very Easy Low Nylon ~100.80%–101.20% 1.0% – 2.0% ~60–80 Very High Medium (flexible, tough) ~100–120c Medium-High Difficult (enclosure) Medium-High

Nylon (PA)

Includes multiple variants such as PA6 (stronger, more hygroscopic), PA12 (easier to print, lower moisture absorption), and filled types like carbon fiber (CF) and glass fiber (GF). Filled nylons have significantly reduced shrinkage and higher rigidity, making them better for structural parts, but are more brittle and abrasive to nozzles. All nylons are hygroscopic and require thorough drying before printing. I do not suggest Nylon due to the difficulties printing.

docs.title: Shrinkage
docs.text: Use this shrinkage calculator to calibrate your printer and filament before printing.

docs.title: Further reading
docs.text:
• 3D4Create (2024) 3D Printer Shrinkage: A Complete Guide
  • Filament2Print (2023) Shrinkage in 3D Printing: Everything You Need to Know
  • All3DP (2023) ASA vs ABS: Material Properties and Printing Characteristics
  • 3dsourced (2023) PLA, ABS, and PETG Shrinkage: Everything You Need To Know
  • 3dprinteddecor (2026) Stop using the wrong slicer

docs.title: Print Settings
docs.text:
Verify the following settings:

  • 0.20mm Strength@BBL X1C Bambu
  • 0.20mm Standard @some printer

Quality->Layer Height

Setting Value Layer Height 0.2mm

Quality->Line width

Setting Value Default 0.42mm

Quality->Advanced

Setting Value Order of walls Inner/Outer/Inner Only one wall on first layer Check

Strength->Walls

Setting Value Wall loops 4 Embedding the wall into infill Checked

Strength->Sparse infill

Setting Value Sparse infill density 40% Sparse infill pattern Locked zag, gyroid, cubic

Strength->Top/bottom shells

Setting Value Bottom shell layers 5

Speed->Other layers speed

This will keep the color consistent

Setting Value Outer wall 50mm/s

docs.title: Printing
docs.text:
When you layout the parts I suggest printing only one part at a time. That way if it the room is too cold and the parts curl up you are not wasting a lot of filament.

Info

If you are having problems with lifting edges I suggest the following settings under Others->Bed adhesion.

Setting Value Skirt Loops 1 Skirt type Per object Skirt distance 5mm Skirt Height 50 Brim type Outer brim Only Draft shield Disabled (its too much) Brim type Outer Brim only Brim Width 5mm (default) Brim-Object gap 0.1mm (default)

This will create a mini draft shield that should keep the corners from curling unless your workspace is really cold.

docs.title: Extra Parts
docs.text:
If you don't want to buy pins, couplers, idlers and pulleys and rails you can print them. I printed them as place holders on my build until my parts came in. I have not had a chance to determine the longevity of these parts, although I doubt the rails will be sufficient for any use other than observation.

  • Rails
  • Couplers
  • Pulleys
  • Idlers
  • Pins (link coming soon) - Print horizontal with ASA and you can not break them.

docs.title: Customization
docs.text:
You can down load different inserts to customize your Rook.

  • Hexmod
  • Vanitymod
